text.title: API Contract-First Development Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every api-contract-first artefact must obey. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Server handlers + client SDKs MUST be generated from the OpenAPI spec, not hand-written and reverse-documented. PRs that change the API without changing the spec are blocked.

rationale.source: OpenAPI Initiative + Stripe / GitHub API guidance

rule.rationale: Two sources of truth diverge; consumers debug against the docs while production behaves differently.

rule.statement: CI MUST include an oasdiff (or equivalent) step that fails on undeclared breaking changes between the PR's spec and the base branch's spec.
rule.rationale: Without the gate, contract-first decays to code-first within a quarter; the spec becomes a stale README.

rule.statement: Every operation MUST have at least one request example AND one 2xx response example; every 4xx/5xx documented status MUST have an example body.
rule.rationale: Examples are how consumers (and codegen tools) understand the contract; absent examples force readers to invent their own.

rule.statement: Response schemas MUST set additionalProperties=false by default. additionalProperties=true requires a documented forward-compat reason.

rationale.source: OpenAPI Initiative + JSON Schema strictness guidance

rule.rationale: Open response schemas leak debug fields; clients ship code that depends on undocumented fields then break on the next version.

rule.statement: Spec MUST declare its versioning policy in info.x-versioning (one of url-path / accept-header / header-key) + breaking-change rules.
rule.rationale: Undeclared versioning lets every endpoint diverge; clients cannot predict deprecation windows.
